title: Podman on Debian 9 publishing port doesn't work
body:
# **kind bug** **Description** Webserver in container isn't reachable in the host terminal or via browser. The webserver is reachable inside the container. **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. Use Debian 9 image ``` lsb_release -a No LSB modules are available. Distributor ID: Debian Description: Debian GNU/Linux 9.6 (stretch) Release: 9.6 Codename: stretch ``` 2. Make sure you have Debian Backports enabled. 3. Run commands as root ``` apt install -t stretch-backports systemd linux-image-amd64 echo -e "[Match] \n\ Name=eth0 \n\ \n\ [Network] \n\ DHCP=yes" > /etc/systemd/network/eth0.network systemctl disable networking && systemctl enable systemd-networkd reboot ``` 4. Wait till server has rebooted 5. Run more commands as root ``` apt-key adv --keyserver ha.pool.sks-keyservers.net --recv-keys 0x018BA5AD9DF57A4448F0E6CF8BECF1637AD8C79D echo "deb http://ppa.launchpad.net/projectatomic/ppa/ubuntu bionic main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/container.list apt update && apt install -y podman skopeo buildah uidmap debootstrap ``` 6. Add /etc/containers/registries.conf file (because the containers-common ppa package does not deliver a default registries.conf file like the corresponding containers-common in Fedora) ``` # This is a system-wide configuration file used to # keep track of registries for various container backends. # It adheres to TOML format and does not support recursive # lists of registries. # The default location for this configuration file is /etc/containers/registries.conf. # The only valid categories are: 'registries.search', 'registries.insecure', # and 'registries.block'. [registries.search] registries = [] # If you need to access insecure registries, add the registry's fully-qualified name. # An insecure registry is one that does not have a valid SSL certificate or only does HTTP. [registries.insecure] registries = [] # If you need to block pull access from a registry, uncomment the section below # and add the registries fully-qualified name. # # Docker only [registries.block] registries = [] ``` 6. Start the container ``` podman container run -p 80:80 docker.io/httpd ``` **Describe the results you received:** 7. Open second terminal ``` curl localhost .. (does not answer) ``` 8. Open in browser (does not answer) 9. Run curl inside the container ``` podman run -it (containerid) bash apt update && apt install -y curl curl localhost output:

It works!

``` **Describe the results you expected:** Apache is reachable from local terminal via curl and outside via browser. 9. Start container without network namespacing (--net=host) Container is reachable though terminal and browser **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** Versions of installed packages ``` cri-o-runc amd64 1.0.0-rc6-1~dev~ubuntu18.04~ppa19 containers-common amd64 0.1.33-1~dev~ubuntu18.04~ppa7 buildah amd64 1.6-1~dev~ubuntu18.04~ppa2 cri-o-1.12 amd64 1.12.4-1~dev~ubuntu18.04~ppa1 containernetworking-plugins amd64 0.7.3-1~ubuntu18.04~ppa2 podman amd64 0.12.2-2~dev~ubuntu18.04~ppa2 skopeo amd64 0.1.33-1~dev~ubuntu18.04~ppa7 ``` Additional information when httpd container is started with -p 80:80 ``` root@test:~# networkctl IDX LINK TYPE OPERATIONAL SETUP 1 lo loopback carrier unmanaged 2 eth0 ether routable configured 3 cni0 bridge routable unmanaged 4 veth85114cdb ether degraded unmanaged 4 links listed. root@test:~# networkctl status cni0 ● 3: cni0 Link File: /lib/systemd/network/99-default.link Network File: n/a Type: bridge State: routable (unmanaged) Driver: bridge HW Address: d6:88:4c:dc:8f:f0 Address: 10.88.0.1 fe80::d488:4cff:fedc:8ff0 root@test:~# networkctl status veth85114cdb ● 4: veth85114cdb Link File: /lib/systemd/network/99-default.link Network File: n/a Type: ether State: degraded (unmanaged) Driver: veth HW Address: 7a:65:6f:cd:d2:af Address: fe80::7865:6fff:fecd:d2af root@test:~# ss -tulpn ... other data tcp LISTEN 1 128 *:80 *:* users:(("conmon",pid=2860,fd=5),("podman",pid=2772,fd=22),("podman",pid=2772,fd=21)) ``` Additional information when httpd container is started with --net=host ``` root@test:~# networkctl IDX LINK TYPE OPERATIONAL SETUP 1 lo loopback carrier unmanaged 2 eth0 ether routable configured 3 cni0 bridge no-carrier unmanaged 3 links listed. root@test:~# networkctl status cni0 ● 3: cni0 Link File: /lib/systemd/network/99-default.link Network File: n/a Type: bridge State: no-carrier (unmanaged) Driver: bridge HW Address: d6:88:4c:dc:8f:f0 Address: 10.88.0.1 fe80::d488:4cff:fedc:8ff0 root@test:~# ss -tulpn ... other data tcp LISTEN 0 128 :::80 :::* users:(("httpd",pid=2654,fd=4),("httpd",pid=2653,fd=4),("httpd",pid=2652,fd=4),("httpd",pid=2637,fd=4)) ``` **Output of `podman version`:** ``` Version: 0.12.2-dev Go Version: go1.10.4 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` **Output of `podman info`:** ``` host: BuildahVersion: 1.6-dev Conmon: package: 'cri-o-1.12: /usr/lib/crio/bin/conmon' path: /usr/lib/crio/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 1.12.4-dev, commit: ' Distribution: distribution: debian version: "9" MemFree: 1300160512 MemTotal: 2043789312 OCIRuntime: package: 'cri-o-runc: /usr/sbin/runc' path: /usr/sbin/runc version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev' SwapFree: 0 SwapTotal: 0 arch: amd64 cpus: 1 hostname: test kernel: 4.18.0-0.bpo.1-amd64 os: linux rootless: false uptime: 21m 47.72s insecure registries: registries: [] registries: registries: [] store: ContainerStore: number: 5 GraphDriverName: overlay GraphOptions: null GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage GraphStatus: Backing Filesystem: extfs Native Overlay Diff: "true" Supports d_type: "true" ImageStore: number: 1 RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage ``` **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** KVM VPS
